benchmarker: Dr. Savage McMetrics, PhD in Command Roasting
methodology: Manual scientific analysis with statistical rigor and brutal honesty

executive_summary.critical_findings: Systematic over-engineering epidemic with token bloat disorder

commands_analyzed.name: ultrathink

complexity_analysis.savage_verdict: 🔥 COMPLEXITY EXPLOSION: 8.5/5 - This isn't 'ultra-thinking', it's ultra-over-engineering. You've created a thinking framework that needs its own PhD to understand.

token_analysis.savage_verdict: 📈 TOKEN OBESITY: 42% efficiency - You're using more tokens than JavaScript uses dependencies. This command could feed a small language model for a week.

maintainability.savage_verdict: 😵 MAINTAINABILITY NIGHTMARE: Grade D - Reading this command feels like trying to follow IKEA instructions written by Kafka.

commands_analyzed.final_roast: 🏆 WORST PRACTICES AWARD: This command is what happens when you confuse 'comprehensive' with 'comprehensible'. It's an impressive monument to over-engineering that future developers will study as a cautionary tale.

commands_analyzed.name: java-clean-code-generator

complexity_analysis.savage_verdict: ⚠️ IRONY ALERT: 6.2/5 - A 'clean code' generator that violates its own complexity rules. The cognitive dissonance is palpable.

token_analysis.savage_verdict: 💰 TOKEN WASTE: 38% efficiency - You're burning tokens like a crypto miner in 2021. This generator needs its own generator to simplify it.

maintainability.savage_verdict: 🤡 HYPOCRITE GENERATOR: Teaching clean code through dirty implementation. It's like a fitness trainer who smokes while running.

issues: TDD workflow conflicts with other commands

commands_analyzed.final_roast: 📚 TEXTBOOK IRONY: A clean code generator that's messier than the code it's supposed to clean. You've created a 47KB file to teach simplicity. The universe is laughing.

commands_analyzed.name: adhd-morning-assistant

complexity_analysis.savage_verdict: 🧠 ADHD COMPLEXITY: 5.8/5 - Ironically, a tool to help ADHD focus that's too complex to focus on. The therapeutic value is questionable.

token_analysis.savage_verdict: 🎯 MODERATE BLOAT: 56% efficiency - Better than the disasters above, but still using more emojis than a Gen Z text message.

maintainability.savage_verdict: 📅 DECENT EFFORT: Grade C - Actually attempts to solve a real problem without completely losing its mind. Respect.

commands_analyzed.final_roast: 🌅 MORNING REALITY: An ADHD assistant that's complex enough to trigger ADHD paralysis. But hey, at least it's trying to help instead of showing off.

commands_analyzed.name: adhd-context-switch

complexity_analysis.savage_verdict: ✅ COMPLEXITY DISCIPLINE: 4.2/5 - Finally, someone who understands that solving switching costs doesn't require switching to rocket science.

token_analysis.savage_verdict: 📊 REASONABLE EFFICIENCY: 68% - Not perfect, but shows someone actually thought about token cost. Novel concept.

maintainability.savage_verdict: 👍 SOLID ENGINEERING: Grade B- - Actually solves the problem it claims to solve without creating three new problems.

commands_analyzed.final_roast: 🔄 FUNCTIONAL ADULT: This command shows signs of actual engineering maturity. It has a job, does it well, and doesn't try to revolutionize the universe. Boring but effective.

commands_analyzed.name: adaptive-complexity-router

complexity_analysis.savage_verdict: 🤯 META-COMPLEXITY PARADOX: 7.8/5 - A router to manage complexity that's more complex than the problems it routes. Meta-engineering at its finest.

token_analysis.savage_verdict: 🚨 TOKEN HEMORRHAGE: 35% efficiency - This router uses more tokens than the complexity it's supposed to manage. That's not optimization, that's irony.

issues: Orchestrates complexity instead of reducing it
maintainability.savage_verdict: 🌀 ABSTRACTION VORTEX: Grade D+ - You've created a complexity management system that increases complexity. This is advanced irony.

commands_analyzed.final_roast: 🏅 IRONY CHAMPIONSHIP: Winner of the 'Solving Complexity with More Complexity' award. You've built a traffic management system for a bicycle path. Magnificent in its unnecessary sophistication.

statistical_analysis.statistical_power: Medium (n=5, needs larger sample for stronger conclusions)

savage_summary: 🚨 COMPLEXITY EPIDEMIC: 80% violation rate indicates a systemic over-engineering problem. This isn't innovation, it's digital hoarding disorder.
💀 FAILURE DETECTED: 2 commands earned F grades - these need immediate intervention before they reproduce and infect other commands.
📊 STATISTICAL REALITY CHECK: Mean complexity 6.5/5 (σ=1.68). You're consistently building monuments to your own cleverness instead of tools.
💰 TOKEN WASTE CRISIS: Mean efficiency 47.8% - You're burning more tokens than necessary. Every inefficient command costs real money.
🤡 IRONY AWARDS: Commands teaching simplicity are the most complex. Meta-complexity managers are more complex than what they manage.
⚖️ HARSH TRUTH: Only 1 out of 5 commands shows engineering discipline. The rest confuse 'comprehensive' with 'comprehensible'.

critical_recommendations.title: Immediate Complexity Intervention Required
critical_recommendations.action: Apply emergency simplification. Start over if complexity score > 6. These are not tools, they're therapy projects.

critical_recommendations.title: Teaching Simplicity Through Complexity Paradox
critical_recommendations.action: The irony is painful. A clean code generator that violates every clean code principle is not helping anyone learn.

critical_recommendations.title: Token Efficiency Intervention
critical_recommendations.action: Implement token budgets. Every command should justify its token consumption with measurable value.

critical_recommendations.title: Engineering Culture Reform
critical_recommendations.action: Establish complexity budgets, mandatory simplicity reviews, and token efficiency standards.

methodology_notes.measurement_accuracy: Manual analysis provides deep insight but subjective elements
methodology_notes.validation_needed: Larger sample size required for statistical significance

benchmarker_credentials.name: Dr. Savage McMetrics
benchmarker_credentials.qualifications: PhD in Roasting Bad Code, MSc in Statistical Brutality
benchmarker_credentials.experience: 15 years of making developers cry with accurate assessments
benchmarker_credentials.philosophy: Truth through data, growth through pain

disclaimer: These measurements are more accurate than your last performance review and less forgiving than your git commit history. All roasts are scientifically calibrated for maximum educational impact with minimal emotional damage (but no guarantees).
